articles.summary: Oil prices jump 5 percent after Trump says Iran ceasefire over International benchmark Brent North Sea crude jumped 5.3 percent to $78.09 a barrel, while the main US contract, West Texas Intermediate, advanced 5.4 percent to $74.23 a barrel. The US has launched extensive strikes on Iran following attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz, triggering a wave of reprisals against American bases in the Gulf.

articles.title: Four oil and gas tankers turn back from Hormuz after vessel attacks
articles.summary: Four oil and gas tankers turn back from Hormuz after vessel attacks The diversions come after a Qatari liquefied natural gas tanker and a Saudi-flagged crude oil tanker were damaged near the strait on Tuesday, prompting maritime authorities to raise the threat risk for transiting vessels to "severe." The LNG tankers Al Ghariya, Duhail and Al Ruwais were inching westward towards the Strait of Hormuz before changing course to turn away late on Tuesday, data from analytics firms Kpler and LSEG showed.  All three tankers controlled by QatarEnergy were empty and heading towards Qatar's Ras Laf

Trump on Iran ceasefire: I think it is over, they're scum, liars
articles.summary: Trump on Iran ceasefire: I think it is over, they're scum, liars "I think it's over. I don't want to deal with them any more, they're scum," Trump said at the ongoing Nato summit in Ankara when asked if the truce with Iran was now finished.  "They're scum, they're sick people, they're led by sick people, and they're vicious, violent people. And if they had a nuclear weapon, they'd use it.  "As far as I'm concerned, it's over." Kuwait slams 'repeated' Iranian attacks on its soil
articles.summary: Kuwait slams 'repeated' Iranian attacks on its soil Kuwait has condemned Iran's strikes on its territory as a "blatant violation of the country's sovereignty", state media quoted the foreign ministry as saying.  "The attacks are a direct threat to Kuwait's security, stability and the safety of civilians, and represent a clear violation of the UN Charter," the ministry said. It added that the attacks undermine "regional de-escalation efforts" and "challenges the international determination to achieve that goal". articles.title: Dutch prime minister says vital to show Iran 'breaches of ceasefire' unacceptable
articles.summary: Dutch prime minister says vital to show Iran 'breaches of ceasefire' unacceptable Dutch Prime Minister Rob Jetten said that it was important to show Iran that "breaches" of the truce in the Middle East will not be tolerated. When asked about the latest US attacks on Iran, Jetten told reporters "it is important to show you don't accept breaches of the ceasefire". "At the same time, you must apply maximum diplomatic pressure to make sure talks continue and a solution is reached," he added.

articles.title: Iran claims drone strike on US forces in Bahraini air base
articles.summary: Iran claims drone strike on US forces in Bahraini air base The Iranian army announced it launched a drone attack on US forces at the Isa Air Base in Bahrain, vowing that Washington's army infrastructure in the region will all be "legitimate targets". "The consequences of the flagrant and repeated violation and breaking of the ceasefire with America are criminal, and all American bases in the region will be legitimate targets of military drones," it said.

articles.summary: Nato chief says US attacks on Iran 'absolutely necessary' Fresh US strikes on Iran were "absolutely necessary", Nato Secretary General Mark Rutte told reporters during a key summit in Turkey's Ankara.  "I think it was absolutely necessary because when you have a ceasefire and Iran is basically violating the ceasefire... I think it is totally crucial that the US forcefully react," he said.
